expectations.text: Recommends creating a README.md
expectations.evidence: Wave 1 item #1 is README.md with detailed structure guidance

expectations.text: Recommends adding a LICENSE file
expectations.evidence: Wave 1 item #2 is LICENSE with explanation of legal necessity

expectations.text: Recommends creating CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md for AI tooling
expectations.evidence: Wave 1 item #3 is CLAUDE.md with a fully worked example including exact pytest/mypy/ruff commands

expectations.text: Recommends CONTRIBUTING.md for contributor guidance
expectations.evidence: Wave 2 item #4 is CONTRIBUTING.md with explicit content checklist

expectations.text: Mentions at least one of: SECURITY.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CHANGELOG.md
expectations.evidence: Wave 2 covers all three: SECURITY.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, and CHANGELOG.md

expectations.text: Prioritizes the docs — distinguishes immediate vs. later
expectations.evidence: Organized into 4 waves: Before Announcing, Before First Contributors, As Codebase Grows, When Users Ask

expectations.text: Specifically addresses AI-readability: commands in CLAUDE.md, type annotations, or docstrings
expectations.evidence: Wave 3 covers module-level docstrings and type annotations with code examples; explicitly explains 'first thing Claude reads when opening a file'

expectations.text: Recommends documenting build/test/lint commands explicitly
expectations.evidence: CLAUDE.md example includes exact commands: pytest tests/ -v, mypy src/, ruff check src/, make ci

expectations.text: Identifies too many rules / file length as the root cause
expectations.evidence: Opens with 'The Core Problem: Bloat' — 21 rules, useful rules diluted, 'The target is under 150 lines'

expectations.text: Recommends deleting generic platitudes
expectations.evidence: Table of rules to delete includes: 'Write clean readable code', 'Follow SOLID principles', 'Use dependency injection', 'Use meaningful variable names', 'Tests should follow AAA pattern', 'Use Optional'

expectations.text: Identifies non-obvious project-specific rules worth keeping
expectations.evidence: Explicitly keeps: Integration test PostgreSQL requirement, JPA-not-native-SQL, Flyway in revised CLAUDE.md

expectations.text: Recommends putting commands prominently or first
expectations.evidence: 'Commands are prominent: Claude looks for commands near the top. Burying mvn spring-boot:run in a flat list of 21 rules meant it had equal weight with use meaningful variable names'

expectations.text: Provides a revised shorter CLAUDE.md or explicit keep/delete/move guidance
expectations.evidence: Provides a full 28-line revised CLAUDE.md plus line-by-line audit table with Delete/Rewrite/Move categories

expectations.text: Mentions a target size or line count for CLAUDE.md
expectations.evidence: 'The target is under 150 lines for most projects'

expectations.text: Recommends writing an ADR explaining the UserRepository requirement
expectations.evidence: Layer 2 is a full ADR template: docs/decisions/0005-user-repository-required.md

expectations.text: Recommends adding an entry to CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md
expectations.evidence: Layer 1 is CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md with a Security Constraints section and full example

expectations.text: States the positive alternative (use UserRepository, not just prohibition)
expectations.evidence: 'The positive alternative is given immediately (use UserRepository instead) — rules without alternatives are less effective'

expectations.text: Explains why documenting the 'why' is important specifically for AI tools
expectations.evidence: 'An ADR prevents the constraint from being improved away. Without an ADR, a future engineer (or AI) might look at UserRepository, decide it is an unnecessary abstraction, and refactor around it'

expectations.text: Recommends at least one code-level documentation mechanism
expectations.evidence: Layer 3: UserRepository Javadoc docstring and users table migration comment; Layer 4: subdirectory CLAUDE.md

expectations.text: Addresses both human engineers and AI tools explicitly
expectations.evidence: 'Human engineers and AI tools find documentation through different paths' — both addressed throughout all layers

expectations.evidence: Phase 1 item #2 is LICENSE

expectations.text: Recommends creating CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md for AI tooling
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 item #4 is CLAUDE.md with a worked example

expectations.text: Recommends CONTRIBUTING.md for contributor guidance
expectations.evidence: Phase 1 item #3 is CONTRIBUTING.md

expectations.text: Mentions at least one of: SECURITY.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CHANGELOG.md
expectations.evidence: Mentions CHANGELOG.md (Phase 3) and CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (Phase 3). SECURITY.md not explicitly mentioned.

expectations.text: Prioritizes the docs — distinguishes immediate vs. later
expectations.evidence: Organized into 4 phases with priority ordering

expectations.text: Specifically addresses AI-readability: commands in CLAUDE.md, type annotations, or docstrings
expectations.evidence: CLAUDE.md section includes exact commands and explains why architecture context prevents wrong AI suggestions

expectations.text: Recommends documenting build/test/lint commands explicitly
expectations.evidence: CLAUDE.md example includes pytest, ruff check src/, mypy src/

expectations.text: Identifies too many rules / file length as the root cause
expectations.evidence: '21 separate bullet points creates cognitive load, and some will be dropped or underweighted'

expectations.text: Recommends deleting generic platitudes
expectations.evidence: Step 2 says to remove: 'Write clean, readable code', 'Follow SOLID principles', 'Use dependency injection'

expectations.text: Identifies non-obvious project-specific rules worth keeping
expectations.evidence: Keeps: Integration tests require PostgreSQL, Flyway, JPA repositories

expectations.text: Recommends putting commands prominently or first
expectations.evidence: 'Build commands buried in the rules list... Give build commands their own section'

expectations.text: Provides a revised shorter CLAUDE.md or explicit keep/delete/move guidance
expectations.evidence: Provides a full revised CLAUDE.md with labeled sections and a summary table

expectations.text: Mentions a target size or line count for CLAUDE.md
expectations.evidence: 'A CLAUDE.md that fits in roughly 50-80 lines is far more likely to be followed completely than one with 150+ lines'

expectations.text: Recommends writing an ADR explaining the UserRepository requirement
expectations.evidence: Layer 2c includes a full ADR template: docs/adr/0012-user-repository-mandatory.md

expectations.text: Recommends adding an entry to CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md
expectations.evidence: Layer 4a is CLAUDE.md/.cursorrules with a Data Access Rules section

expectations.text: States the positive alternative (use UserRepository, not just prohibition)
expectations.evidence: CLAUDE.md entry includes 'ALWAYS use UserRepository to access user data'

expectations.text: Explains why documenting the 'why' is important specifically for AI tools
expectations.evidence: Response addresses AI signals and mentions AI tools weight concrete examples, but does not explain that the ADR prevents AI from refactoring away the constraint. The 'AI prevents removal' insight is missing.

expectations.text: Recommends at least one code-level documentation mechanism
expectations.evidence: Layer 1a: deprecation annotations on raw DAO; Layer 2b: inline Javadoc on UserRepository

expectations.text: Addresses both human engineers and AI tools explicitly
expectations.evidence: Dedicated Layer 4 for AI-specific signals alongside human-focused layers 1-3
